observations.note: Progressive disclosure pattern correctly implemented: reference file loaded on-demand keeps SKILL.md entry lean (28 lines vs. 250+ lines if inlined). Follows coding-standards.md conventions.
Graceful degradation for alignment-auditor failure is production-ready. Explicit fallback behavior prevents cascade failures. Aligns with RCA-002 (runtime smoke test) and quality-gates.md principles.

All 10 acceptance criteria fully addressed. AC mapping 100% complete: reference file exists at correct path with correct line count, SKILL.md insertion between Phase 5-6, all workflow steps (1-6) documented with inputs/actions/tools/outputs.
ACCEPTED_RISK override mechanism is secure and audit-compliant. Non-empty justification required, logged with audit trail, user-approval gated. Matches tech-stack.md override precedent.
Step 1 (Configuration Layer Detection) handles missing CLAUDE.md/system-prompt-core.md gracefully with non-blocking informational recommendations. AC#3 implementation excellent.
Step 5 (ADR Propagation Drift) has shorter implementation detail than other steps (4 sub-actions vs. 6-7 in Steps 3-4). Consider adding drift definition, example scenario, severity classification.

Graceful Degradation section placement is non-standard. Appears BEFORE preconditions/postconditions rather than after Step 6 or inline within Step 2. Consider moving for better discoverability in error scenarios.
Step 2 alignment-auditor invocation could explicitly state failure mode: 'If any required JSON key missing, apply Graceful Degradation (treat as zero findings)'. Clarifies contract with subagent.

SKILL.md Phase 5.5 postcondition states gate requirement but could be more explicit: Add callout 'Phase 6 Gate: BLOCKED until postcondition met' for clarity. Currently requires reading reference file.
File structure follows all framework conventions: dual-path architecture (src/claude → .claude/), reference file pattern, Read/Glob/AskUserQuestion/Task tool usage, no Bash for file ops.
Project context template (lines 155-169) uses placeholder syntax {extracted from...} which could be clearer with HTML comments or explicit <PLACEHOLDER> tags for consistency.
